subject: [Pre-read] Reg CB LFF/ CNLS/ Projects Monthly Update - Feb 2026

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

Please find attached slides for our discussion tomorrow, let me know for
any questions.

Thank you.

-- 
*Jason YU*
*Regional CB | MY & SG CB*
*Mobile*: +65 90284825
*Email*: jason.yu@shopee.com

content.question:
Thanks.



Slide 4

   - How is FBS vs 3PF situation in each market?

Which is growing faster
   for each market?
How can we make sure that FBS grows faster than 3PF?

Slide 5

   - What is each market doing for penetration program right now?
- Which market is working vs not working?

Slide 8

   - How do we calculate buyer facing right now?

For eg. do we calculate
   all vouchers?
Or only rebaetes/vouchers that is specific to help FBS grow?

- Seller facing CPO feels odd here (for eg. how can SG be lowest in
   seller facing if WH cost is the highest). Does this include SLS vs FBS
   difference?
Are we following Faye’s definition?

Slide 9

   - Do we have the latest view of Jan/Feb?

To see if we are trending in
   the right direction?

And we should be measuring similar to how Faye is
   measuring it



Slide 14

   - Why is the conversion to addressable so different for TH and PH?

Slide 15

   - How will we solve for SIP?
Seems like big issue for SG and MY?
- What is the ADIS requirement for each market right now?

Slide 21

   - I do not understand what does CB SCS got to do with the viability of
   this



Slide 22

   - We need to include Seller own WH store/pick &pack cost for SLS?
- What does FCL mean?
- Our CB FBS cost for Dec’26 looks odd?
For eg. MY should not be 0.4?

Slide 56

   - Can we discuss this further?

And do we have view of other markets
   outside SG/MY?

Hi all,

Please see follow up actions from our catchup today, let me know for any
questions.

No. Topic Action Items PIC DDL
1 Penetration program Prepare summary on programs we offer for FBS, and
penetration program effectiveness across markets Jason 16 Feb
2 PH CB FBS target setting PH has high potential to do more FBS. Re-assess
PH CB FBS targets after WH issues are resolved. Check on timeline Jason 4
March
3 Anbo WH Add in SLS seller pick and pack cost saving to the true cost
comparison table
Think through ways to lower Anbo model with options. Assess if can further
lower down Anbo staging true cost, such as 1) requesting seller to ship
specific amount to reduce staging CPI, 2) studying Amazon model Jason 4
March
5 Counter TB To build a U/E view of Counter TB program (split by SWARM and
non-SWARM, and SWARM by existing shops and new shops). Jason 20 Feb
6 Seaway Cost Optimization To review SG seaway channel combined seller P&L
(MP + SLS) and decide if channel can continue to operate with existing cost
and rate structure. Phyllis 4 March
7 Shopbot Shift the focus to prioritizing ShopBot adoption among CB sellers
with poor CSAT and low chat response rates, rather than targeting overall
CB adoption. Jason/Sophie/Joanne Ongoing

Hi Ian

It is not about getting seller to ship specific amount but for us to treat


Got it. We are checking on the ops model and cost that MY WH replenishing
for Satellite WH. will get back with options and pros/cons per each option,
including the option that ship it to local markets instead of breaking it
up


On the other hand, please find below update for the follow up items on
penetration program


Recap on 2026 seller program

[image: image.png]

Below please find the penetration program initial effectiveness assessment
by market

[image: image.png]

Overall 47% of penetration program sellers increased FBS penetration vs
Dec’25, outperforming non-program sellers with only 34% sellers increased
FBS penetration

   -

   SG penetration program 46% seller increased FBS penetration vs
   non-program only 29%
   -

   MY penetration program 44% seller increased FBS penetration vs
   non-program only 34%
   -

   PH penetration program 49% seller increased FBS penetration vs
   non-program only 36%


For next step, we will further push sellers who not yet increased FBS
penetration via optimizing program mechanism based on CN and KR seller
feedback

   -

   Mechanism change: starting from Feb, we would breakdown quarterly
   targets into monthly targets to make targets more attainable, especially
   those sellers who need lead time to prepare new sku inventory and inbound

Thank you

Best, Angela

content.question: are we happy with the progress of program pitching?

content.answer:
Hi Ian,

In short, yes we saw
1) High seller adoption of the penetration program (>90%)
2) Meaningful improvements to FBS ADO% to Shop ADO (34.7%, +3pp MoM, higher
vs Nov/Dec '25) and are on track to our target.
However, there is room to optimize further based on feedback we have
collected from sellers, and we will continue to monitor seller adoption at
the seller level closely.

More details below on what we will do differently:
- Program Mechanics: We currently give 1 quarter for sellers to hit the
target FBS ADO%, and as long as they hit this target, they will enjoy
incentive for the rest of the quarter if they maintain above target.
- Issue: For the sellers who did not adopt the program/fail the targets,
they think the targets we set are stretched and they only get rewarded at
the end of the quarter for hitting the target penetration, effectively
cutting down the duration they enjoy the incentive should they only hit the
target at the end of the quarter.
- Next steps: To improve adoption for these sellers, we will break down
quarterly targets to monthly targets so that sellers who need time to
prepare new SKU inventory are incentivised to grow as well. This is aligned
with CN team and we will monitor the improvement in adoption/effectiveness
of this program tweak.
